column4: Notes

Source:setFilter( settings ) in Love2D currently only supports three basic filters: HighPass, LowPass, and BandPass in a very limited way. AllPass is not one of the supported filters currently. However, I've recently found this LOVE2D sound processing library that has support for AllPass and more customization for BandPass, HighPass and LowPass https://love2d.org/forums/viewtopic.php?t=82944.

column2: Processing’s filters are more user-friendly (named classes with freq(), res()). In L5sound we would likely create analogous classes (e.g. L5.LowPass), but internally call source:setFilter({type="lowpass", ...}). The mapping of parameters is not one-to-one (Love uses filter gains rather than direct Q factor), so some translation logic is needed. AllPass (phase shift) has no direct equivalent; could be omitted or simulated by combining delays.
column4: Source:setFilter( settings ) in Love2D seems to only supports three basic filters: HighPass, LowPass, and BandPass in a very limited way. You can only pass the volume, highgain and lowgain values through the "settings" table allowing little control. Researching https://love2d.org/forums/viewtopic.php?t=82944 for more support to achieve feature parity

column2: Some Processing parameters may not exactly match Love’s (e.g. Love’s reverb has roomSize, damp, tilt etc.). But overall, core features exist.
column4: Delay (called echo in LOVE2D) is one of the built in effects with a good deal of support. you can define effects with love.audio.setEffect(name, {type = <EffectType>, ...}) and apply them to sources with source:setEffect(name) and with even more customization parameters than Processing Sound. There's also this example of ModularLove, a port of ModularPlay that has some great examples of these effects: https://github.com/orllewin/love2d_modular_love?tab=readme-ov-file
Similar to BandPass
Similar to BandPass

Reverb is similar to Delay (or echo in LOVE2D) in that it also has a good deal of support already within Love2D. Although the exact implementation isn't the same between Processing and Love2D, I believe a feature-parity implementation is achieveable considering the amount of customization supported

column4: There could be three possible types of sources in L5. Native Love2D has SoundFile, which we can access the sound data via Decoder.read(). There's also the Oscillators/Noise that we custom implement and finally there's the recording input from RecordingDevices, which gives us the PCM data via RecordingDevice:getData( )

column2: Love can access the mic via something like RecordingDevice = love.audio.getRecordingDevices()[1] and RecordingDevice:start(). The audio comes in as PCM; you can pull data using device:getSoundData(). (This is an advanced feature of Love 11+.) In practice, L5sound could wrap the first microphone device as AudioIn, and feed its PCM into Love’s audio output or into analysis objects.
column4: While Love2d does store audio input device capable of recording sounds in Recording Device, we might need something like a wrapper implemented around Source. We then can use the methods available to Source to easily implement the features in Processing Sound, but that's only if we can get it to wrap nicely around Source in Love2d

column2: although we can access the recording devices via love.audio.getRecordingDevices(). Love2D 11.5 currently does not support access to the playback devices. There's been discussion about it going far back to 2022 witht this issue https://github.com/love2d/love/issues/1537 and since then it's been implemented and merged but only to the 12.0ver which isn't officially released yet in this PR https://github.com/love2d/love/pull/1805.
column4: We can possibly look at the implementation of access to playback devices in the mentioned PR for reference and implement our own version of it or wrap around/implement in a more native Processinglike way

Difference between the source:seek() used here to implement jumpFrame() and jump() is source:seek() sets the currently playing position of the Source. With the queueable source type, this method can only work on the currently playing buffer.

There is also no Native Love2d Envelope, but I imagine one would implement it in L5 by modulating :setVolume() of a Source over time (e.g. using a table of values and a timer). This is straightforward in Lua. Essentially, L5sound’s Env.play() could start a coroutine or use LOVE’s timer to ramp volume.
